message:
feat(wezterm+watchdog): tick 211 WeztermInterface::watchdog_warnings_with_cx trait sibling + watchdog::check_cx routing (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Follow-up to tick 206. The watchdog::check_cx ping was tick-206
routed through list_panes_with_cx — but the warning_probe at L632
was left on ambient `watchdog_warnings()` with a note that adding
a cx-first sibling required a trait extension across 5 impls.

Tick 211 does that trait extension in the minimum-possible way:

1. **wezterm.rs trait body**: add `watchdog_warnings_with_cx` as a
   `#[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]` method with a default
   impl that delegates to `watchdog_warnings()`. No existing impls
   need to change — all 5 impls inherit the default. Matches the
   exact pattern used for `list_panes_with_cx` (L58) and
   `get_pane_with_cx` (L74) additions from the ft-xbnl0.2.2 era.

2. **watchdog.rs check_cx**: swap L635 from
   `self.wezterm.watchdog_warnings()` to
   `self.wezterm.watchdog_warnings_with_cx(cx)`. The outer
   timeout_with_cx wrapper is unchanged.

This is a narrow, additive trait change. Concrete impls with a
Cx-aware path (e.g. a future WeztermClient override that calls
mux pool _with_cx helpers for telemetry) gain end-to-end cx
threading when they override the default. Today's 5 impls inherit
the default and see identical behavior to the legacy path.

Verification:
- 224 / 224 watchdog:: tests pass under --features asupersync-runtime
- 171 / 171 wezterm:: tests pass (trait extension compiles clean
  across all 5 impls + the trait body default)
via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target).

Closes the remaining tick-206 note about watchdog_warnings trait-level
absence — the `WeztermInterface` cx-first surface now covers every
async method with a sensible default or concrete override.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -629,10 +629,17 @@ impl MuxWatchdog {
 
         let rss_bytes = get_mux_server_rss_with_cx(cx).await;
 
+        // Tick 211 (ft-xbnl0.2.3): route the warning probe through
+        // `watchdog_warnings_with_cx(cx)` (added this tick). Default
+        // trait impl delegates to ambient `watchdog_warnings()`, so
+        // impls without an override see identical behavior; impls
+        // that override get end-to-end cx threading. Previously this
+        // wrapped ambient watchdog_warnings() in timeout_with_cx —
+        // same shape as the ping path before tick 206.
         let warning_probe = match crate::runtime_compat::timeout_with_cx(
             cx,
             self.config.ping_timeout,
-            self.wezterm.watchdog_warnings(),
+            self.wezterm.watchdog_warnings_with_cx(cx),
         )
         .await
         {
